mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in the supply chain, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market, which reduces wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts signals an expansion in the construction sector, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP signals an expansion in the economy, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which reduces wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in the supply chain, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 50% of triggered instances.
